question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user open the maximum number of concurrent gRPC connections (each saturating maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection=5) to exhaust the applicationTaskExecutor thread pool, causing the EntityStakeCalculatorImpl to stall mid-calculation and leave pending staking rewards in an inconsistent state that a subsequent attacker-controlled query can read and exploit?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the gRPC server with long-lived streaming subscriptions (up to maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection=5 per connection) across many connections to starve the applicationTaskExecutor, delaying the NodeStakeUpdateTransactionHandler from publishing NodeStakeUpdatedEvent and preventing pending reward recalculation from completing, causing staking rewards to remain unclaimed?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a code path where the @Qualifier('readOnly') TransactionTemplate is injected into a component that should be using a read-write transaction, causing staking reward transfer records to be silently dropped (not persisted) because the read-only transaction rolls back writes without error?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a gRPC request that causes the read-only TransactionTemplate to be used for a staking reward query at a snapshot timestamp that predates the latest pendingReward update, returning a stale lower reward value that the user then uses to argue for a discrepancy and trigger a re-payout?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of per-IP connection rate limiting in the NettyServerBuilder configuration to open thousands of connections, each consuming up to 5 concurrent calls, exhausting file descriptors and preventing legitimate staking reward claim transactions from being processed by the importer?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared applicationTaskExecutor (no dedicated thread pool for gRPC vs. staking reward calculation) to time a burst of gRPC calls that monopolizes all executor threads precisely when EntityStakeCalculatorImpl.calculate() holds the entity_stake table lock, causing a deadlock that corrupts the pending reward state?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the read-only TransactionTemplate does not enforce a specific transaction isolation level, allowing a dirty-read or non-repeatable-read of the entity_stake table during a concurrent updateEntityStake() call, returning an inflated pendingReward value that the user can screenshot as proof of a higher reward entitlement?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user send malformed gRPC frames that cause the Netty server to allocate large buffers per connection without releasing them, exhausting heap memory and causing the staking reward calculation transaction to fail with an OOM error, leaving entity_stake rows with stale pendingReward values?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maxConnectionAge or keepAlive timeout in the NettyServerBuilder configuration to hold connections open indefinitely, preventing the server from recycling threads and causing the staking reward update pipeline to queue indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger concurrent gRPC calls that each open a read-only transaction against the staking_reward_transfer table at the same snapshot, observe a pending reward amount before it is zeroed out by a payout, and use that observation to submit a duplicate claim on-chain before the mirror node's state is updated?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection is applied per-connection (not globally) by opening many connections to bypass the intended concurrency cap, flooding the applicationTaskExecutor and delaying the staking reward account (0.0.800) balance update that gates reward eligibility?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of explicit transaction timeout on the read-only TransactionTemplate to hold a long-running read transaction open against entity_stake, blocking vacuum/autovacuum on PostgreSQL and causing table bloat that slows down the updateEntityStake() write path, delaying reward distribution?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user send a high volume of ConsensusService subscribe requests that each trigger a database query via the read-only transaction, causing connection pool exhaustion on the gRPC DB pool and preventing the staking reward transfer records from being written by the importer's separate DB connection?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the read-only TransactionTemplate's lack of row-level locking to read staking reward data concurrently with an in-progress insertStakingRewardTransfers() call, observing a partial write where some reward transfers are visible but others are not, leading to an incorrect reward balance calculation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of TLS enforcement in the NettyServerBuilder configuration to perform a man-in-the-middle attack on the gRPC channel, intercepting staking reward query responses and replaying them with modified reward amounts to deceive a downstream client into believing a higher reward is owed?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared applicationTaskExecutor to submit tasks that starve the NodeStakeUpdatedEvent listener, preventing EntityStakeCalculatorImpl from being triggered after a NodeStakeUpdateTransaction, causing the pending reward to remain at the pre-update value and allowing the user to claim a reward based on an outdated (potentially higher) rate?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the read-only TransactionTemplate to repeatedly query the staking_reward_transfer table with different timestamp filters, enumerating all reward payouts for all accounts and building a map of unpaid rewards that can be used to front-run legitimate reward claims?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of request size limits in the NettyServerBuilder configuration to send oversized gRPC messages that cause the server to allocate excessive memory, triggering GC pauses that delay the staking reward calculation and create a window where rewards can be double-counted?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the read-only TransactionTemplate does not set a statement timeout, allowing a slow query against the entity_stake table to hold a database connection open indefinitely, exhausting the connection pool and preventing the staking reward payout transaction from acquiring a connection?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of authentication on the gRPC endpoint to subscribe to topic messages and infer the timing of NodeStakeUpdateTransactions, then time an account staking change to maximize the pending reward captured before the epoch boundary?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the read-only TransactionTemplate's MVCC snapshot to read the entity_stake table at a point in time where the pending_reward field has been incremented but the corresponding staking_reward_transfer record has not yet been inserted, observing an inflated pending reward that does not reflect already-paid amounts?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of flow control configuration in the NettyServerBuilder to send a flood of gRPC requests faster than the server can process them, causing the applicationTaskExecutor queue to grow unboundedly and delaying the processing of staking reward transfer events past the epoch boundary, causing rewards to be miscalculated?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the read-only TransactionTemplate to query the entity_stake table for account 0.0.800 (stakingRewardAccount) and observe the reserved_staking_rewards balance, then use this information to calculate the maximum claimable reward and craft transactions that drain the reward pool before other stakers can claim?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the NettyServerBuilder does not configure maxConnectionIdle to keep zombie connections alive, preventing the server from reclaiming threads and causing the staking reward calculation to be delayed past the 365-day forfeiture window, causing legitimate stakers to lose rewards?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: transactionOperationsReadOnly()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of row-level security on the read-only transaction to enumerate all accounts with non-zero pending_reward values from the entity_stake table via gRPC-triggered queries, identifying high-value targets whose rewards can be front-run on the consensus layer?
